thought.text:
So `deadVar` is computed but only used to write to global `deadVarGlobal` once per loop iteration (which is overwritten next iteration). So the entire deadVar computation chain inside the loop is dead — only the LAST value matters, plus the global is overwritten each iter but only the very final iteration's value is observable.

Hmm wait, the global is overwritten each iteration, so all previous writes are dead. Only the final iteration matters. To eliminate the dead writes inside a loop we need cross-iteration DSE or LICM.

For now let me look at if-combine and bitset for easier wins:
